steps.action: The Bihu dance is an indigenous folk dance from with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: The Bihu dance is an indigenous folk dance from with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Performed in a group, the Bihu dancers are usual
steps.text: Performed in a group, the Bihu dancers are usually young men and women, and the dancing style is characterized by brisk steps and rapid hand movements.

steps.action: The traditional costume of dancers is centered a
steps.text: The traditional costume of dancers is centered around the red color theme, signifying joy and vigour.

steps.action: According to scholars, the Bihu dance has its or
steps.text: According to scholars, the Bihu dance has its origins in ancient fertility cults that were associated with increasing the fertility of the population as well as the land.

steps.action: Traditionally, local farming communities perform
steps.text: Traditionally, local farming communities performed the dance outdoors, in fields, groves, forests or on the banks of rivers, especially under the fig tree.

steps.action: The earliest depiction of Bihu dance is found in
steps.text: The earliest depiction of Bihu dance is found in the 9th-century sculptures found in the Tezpur and Darrang districts of Assam.

steps.action: Gradually, they open up their shoulders and plac
steps.text: Gradually, they open up their shoulders and place their legs slightly apart, adopting the main posture used in the Bihu dance.

steps.action: The festival takes place in mid-April, and the B
steps.text: The festival takes place in mid-April, and the Bihu dance is meant to celebrate and emulate the seasonal spirit, celebrating fertility and passion.

steps.action: Bihu is performed by groups of young men and wom
steps.text: Bihu is performed by groups of young men and women, and in earlier times, it served principally as a courtship dance.

steps.action: Presently, the Bihu dance is a cultural emblem i
steps.text: Presently, the Bihu dance is a cultural emblem in modern-day Assamese society, becoming a symbol of the Assamese cultural identity.

steps.action: The first time that the Bihu dance was performed
steps.text: The first time that the Bihu dance was performed on a stage was in 1962, as part of a cultural event that took place in Guwahati.

steps.action: A group of 11,000 Bihu dancers and drummers part
steps.text: A group of 11,000 Bihu dancers and drummers participated.

steps.action: Other Indian Folk Dances of Various parts of Ind
steps.text: Other Indian Folk Dances of Various parts of India.

steps.action: Rati Bihu : A kind of bihu dance celebrated by P
steps.text: Rati Bihu : A kind of bihu dance celebrated by People in Assam.

steps.action: Bihu is mentioned in the inscriptions of the 14t
steps.text: Bihu is mentioned in the inscriptions of the 14th-century Chutia King Lakshminaryan as well.

steps.action: The women then start to slowly move in tune with
steps.text: The women then start to slowly move in tune with the music by swaying, while bending slightly forward from the waist.

steps.action: Meanwhile, the music played by the men picks up
steps.text: Meanwhile, the music played by the men picks up in temp, leading women to thrust forward their breasts and pelvis, alternatively, to the tune.

steps.action: The use of instruments such as drums and hornpip
steps.text: The use of instruments such as drums and hornpipes is believed to replicate the sound of rain and thunder as a way of invoking actual precipitation.

steps.action: The event took place at Indira Gandhi Athletic S
steps.text: The event took place at Indira Gandhi Athletic Stadium, Guwahati Assam.
